figures.caption: Figure 1: The on-device micro language model µLM initiates the response, which the cloud LLM continues.

Figure 4: Benchmarking micro language models. (a) Five µLM variants by changing hidden size and layers. (b) Overall quantitative score across five benchmarks via lm-eval-harness. (c) Scaling µLM improves overall qualitative score on dialogue-style datasets. For fairness, we compare with models that were trained on SFT data.
Table 4: Detailed results for each of the five benchmark tasks.
Table 5: Handoff correction rate frequency under different number of words committed by the local micro language model.
Table 7: Rubric results on real-user prompts. Each cell reports the mean with standard deviation in parentheses. Avg. is the average across the four rubric scores.
Table 6: Detailed rubric scores for each model across the three datasets and the overall set. CC: ClarityCoherence; Corr: Correctness; Info: Informativeness; Local: LocalHelpfulness; Rel: Relevance.
Figure 2: Example responses of µLM+LLM framework.

Table 1: Main results with different micro model sizes, continuation settings, and max word budgets. Each cell reports the mean with standard deviation in parentheses. Avg. is the average across the four rubric scores.
Table 8: Rubric results on academic datasets. Each cell reports the mean with standard deviation in parentheses. Avg. is the average across the four rubric scores.
Figure 3: Illustration of our three error recovery modes.
